test_cases.title: Hardcoded Secrets
test_cases.description: Search the codebase for keywords like `password`, `api_key`, `secret`.

test_cases.title: Insecure Deserialization
test_cases.description: Look for use of `pickle` in Python, `ObjectInputStream` in Java, etc.

test_cases.title: Semgrep Scan
test_cases.description: Run Semgrep with a specific configuration to find vulnerabilities.

test_cases.title: Bandit Scan
test_cases.description: Run Bandit to find common security issues in Python code.

test_cases.title: Insecure randomness / predictable tokens
test_cases.description: Identify use of non-cryptographic RNGs for session IDs, password reset tokens, or crypto keys.
steps: Search for usages of `rand()`, `Random()`, `Math.random()`, or timestamps assigned to security tokens.
Trace code to see if outputs are used for session identifiers, password reset links, or crypto keys.
Recommend cryptographically secure RNGs (e.g., `secrets` in Python, `SecureRandom` in Java).

test_cases.title: Server-side request forgery sinks in code
test_cases.description: Review code for HTTP client usage that fetches user-provided URLs without allowlists or validation.
steps: Locate wrappers around `requests.get`, `HttpClient`, `URL.openStream`, etc., where parameters originate from user input.
Check for SSRF mitigations (allowlists, IP blocking, DNS pinning).
Flag missing validations and suggest canonicalization plus network-layer egress controls.

test_cases.title: Sensitive logging and tracing
test_cases.description: Ensure logs do not store secrets (passwords, tokens, PII) or stack traces in production builds.
steps: Search for logging statements referencing `password`, `Authorization`, or entire request bodies.
Review exception handlers to verify stack traces are sanitized before reaching logs/users.
Recommend structured logging with redaction and environment-based log levels.
